title: [Task Sprint] QA Testing Report — Villume Admin

issues.main_description_html:
Epic C — Terms (/term-of-service)

Slice the Terms page — also builds the shared legal layout (sticky TOC, numbered sections, contact callout, back-to-top).

Scope

  • Legal hero (breadcrumb, effective date), sticky TOC, 8 numbered sections, callout, back-to-top.
  • Light + dark; responsive (TOC collapses on mobile). Markup only.

Acceptance criteria

  • Matches Terms.html in both themes, all breakpoints.

Testing scenario (QA)

  1. Compare to Terms.html on desktop & mobile.
  2. Dark mode styling correct.
  3. Mobile → TOC behaves correctly (collapse/stack).

Demo by Developer

QA Tester Summary — Issue #1614: Terms page (/term-of-service)

Suite: qa_testing/scripts/record-issue-1614-dev-flow.cjs (Playwright · Chrome headed · FFmpeg desktop recording)
Env: Development https://dev.villume.com/term-of-service

Test Scenario & Result

# Box Scenario Given → Expected Matrix Result
1 White terms_page_renders open Terms URL → legal page renders with Terms of Use heading TP Pass
2 White legal_layout_content page loaded → TOC has 8 entries, document has 8 numbered sections, contact callout and back-to-top are present TP Pass
3 Gray toc_navigation click TOC item → page scrolls to matching section and active state updates TP Pass
4 Gray theme_support switch light/dark → Terms page remains readable and styled correctly Desktop light + desktop dark Pass
5 Gray responsive_mobile emulate mobile viewport while Chrome remains maximized → mobile layout stacks correctly Mobile light + mobile dark Pass
6 Black rendered_text_encoding inspect rendered body text → no mojibake/encoding corruption is visible TN Pass
7 Task Sprint #1614 acceptance Terms.html parity target → hero, sticky/mobile TOC, 8 sections, contact callout, back-to-top, responsive and dark mode verified TP + TN Pass

Frontend Status & Review

Flow: https://dev.villume.com/term-of-service → Angular static route term-of-service → ServiceComponent

Area Expected Review
Route /term-of-service opens Terms page on dev Verified
Hero Breadcrumb, legal eyebrow, effective date, Terms of Use heading Verified
TOC 8 entries, active section state, click-to-scroll behavior Verified
Legal body 8 numbered sections and readable legal copy Verified
Callout Contact callout and back-to-top control present Verified
Theme Light and dark modes render correctly Verified
Responsive Mobile layout stacks correctly without resizing the Chrome window; only the web viewport is emulated Verified
Evidence Video records visible Chrome window including address bar Verified

Response Code & Review

Request Response Content-Type Review
GET https://dev.villume.com/term-of-service 200 OK text/html; charset=utf-8 Verified

Code Review

File Review
src/app/routes/static.routes.ts term-of-service is registered as a static route and lazy-loads ServiceComponent.
src/app/pages/service/service.component.ts TOC click handling, back-to-top, reveal behavior, and scroll-spy are guarded for browser-only execution and cleaned up on destroy.
src/app/pages/service/service.component.html Terms markup contains the required hero, 8 TOC entries, 8 legal sections, contact callout, and back-to-top control.
src/app/pages/service/service.component.scss Terms styles cover desktop sticky TOC, mobile stacked TOC, dark/light theme tokens, and responsive callout behavior.

Bug Notes

  • None found for the tested Terms page flow.
  • Console warnings observed were limited to Google Sign-In/FedCM messages and were not related to the Terms page.

Desktop screenshot - light mode:

Mobile screenshot - dark mode:

Mobile screenshot - light mode:

issue-1614-final-address-bar.mp4

Epic C - 404 (/404)

Slice the 404 page.

Scope

  • Big "404", message, Back to home + Go back buttons, recovery chips (Templates, Exhibitions, Tutorials, Contact).
  • Light + dark; responsive. Markup only.

Acceptance criteria

  • Matches 404.html in both themes, all breakpoints.

Testing scenario (QA)

  1. Compare to 404.html.
  2. Dark mode + mobile correct.

Demo by Developer

QA Tester Summary - Issue #1612: 404 page (/404)

Suite: qa_testing/tests/e2e/not-found/issue_1612_404_page.spec.ts (Playwright - Chromium)
Env: Dev https://dev.villume.com/ - route /404
Evidence recording: one clean Playwright evidence run only. OS-screen MP4 starts before the first Playwright step and stops after the final step, Chrome is headed/maximized with address bar visible, desktop uses the real maximized browser, mobile uses responsive emulation, and Playwright actions use slowMo: 2000ms.
Screenshot policy: screenshots are generated during the same Playwright run at the end of every test.step() and saved under qa_testing/reports/screenshots/.

Test Scenario & Result

# Box Scenario Given -> Expected Matrix Result
1 White desktop_light_404_layout /404 desktop light mode -> big 404, message, buttons, recovery chips visible and linked correctly TP Pass
2 White desktop_dark_404_layout /404 desktop dark mode -> same 404 recovery layout visible without horizontal overflow TP Pass
3 Black mobile_light_responsive /404?qa=issue-1612-mobile-light responsive mobile light mode -> renders without horizontal overflow TP Pass
4 Black mobile_dark_responsive /404?qa=issue-1612-mobile-dark responsive mobile dark mode -> renders without horizontal overflow TP Pass
5 Task Sprint #1612 acceptance markup/style task -> light/dark desktop and responsive mobile 404 recovery page verified TP Pass

Backend Status Code & Review

Flow: https://dev.villume.com/404 -> Angular route 404 -> NotFoundComponent

Backend result Status code Frontend outcome Review
Direct /404 page load page served by app shell 404 component renders Verified
API dependency Not applicable No backend API validation required for markup-only slicing task Not in scope
Unknown arbitrary route fallback Not part of issue scope Automation stays focused on direct /404 acceptance criteria Not in scope

Bug notes

  • None found for the scoped /404 slicing acceptance criteria.
  • This is intentionally a non-functional UI/slicing automation test.
  • Passed and bug evidence policy: this task result includes per-step screenshots plus one playable MP4 recording of the full execution.

issue-1612-playwright-full-run.mp4

Epic A — Auth · Signup (/signup)

Slice the full-page Signup design (reuses the shared auth layout).

Scope

  • Two-column layout; form: first/last name, email, password (show/hide, min 8), Terms+Privacy consent checkbox, reCAPTCHA placeholder, Create account, Google, "Log in" link.
  • Light + dark; responsive. Markup only.

Acceptance criteria

  • Matches the new Signup design in both themes, all breakpoints.

Testing scenario (QA)

  1. Compare /signup to Signup.html on desktop & mobile.
  2. Toggle password show/hide.
  3. Dark mode styling correct.
  4. Mobile → fields/columns stack correctly.

Demo by Developer

QA Tester Summary - Issue #1600: Signup page (/signup)

Suite: qa_testing/scripts/record-issue-1600-dev-flow.cjs (Playwright - Chrome headed/maximized - FFmpeg desktop recording)
Env: Development https://dev.villume.com/signup
Flow: Open /signup, click Get started, then verify the signup dialog/form.

Test Scenario & Result

# Box Scenario Given -> Expected Matrix Result
1 White signup_entry_flow open /signup + click Get started -> signup form appears TP Pass
2 White signup_form_content form opened -> first/last name, email, password, consent checkbox, Create account, Google sign-up, and Log in link are visible TP Pass
3 White recaptcha_markup form opened -> reCAPTCHA container exists for signup verification TP Pass with note: external widget was not visibly rendered in the captured frame
4 Gray password_toggle click show/hide password -> password input toggles between hidden and visible TP Pass
5 Gray theme_support switch light/dark -> signup form remains readable and styled correctly Desktop light + desktop dark Pass
6 Gray responsive_mobile emulate mobile viewport while Chrome stays maximized -> signup form remains usable in mobile responsive view Mobile light + mobile dark Pass
7 Black password_min_length issue scope requires password minimum 8 characters -> implementation enforces/copies minimum 8 FN Fail
8 Task Sprint #1600 acceptance Signup.html parity target -> signup form, responsive states, dark mode, and evidence verified TP + FN Partial Pass

Frontend Status & Review

Flow: https://dev.villume.com/signup -> Get started button -> app-sign-up dialog.

Area Expected Actual Review
Entry route /signup can start the signup flow URL remains /signup; clicking Get started opens the signup dialog Pass
Signup component app-sign-up / .auth-form visible Rendered after Get started click Pass
Form fields First name, last name, email, password, consent checkbox visible 5 visible inputs detected: first, last, email, password, checkbox Pass
Create account CTA Visible and disabled/enabled according to form state Visible; disabled until valid form + captcha Pass
Google sign-up Google sign-up option visible Visible as Daftar dengan Google Pass
Log in link Login path for existing user visible Visible as Already have an account? Log in Pass
Password toggle Show/hide control available and functional Toggle button changes password input visibility Pass
Responsive Mobile signup layout remains usable Verified in mobile light and mobile dark screenshots using viewport emulation while browser stays maximized Pass
Evidence Video records visible Chrome window including address bar Verified from extracted frame: address bar shows dev.villume.com/signup Pass

Response Code & Review

Request Response Content-Type Body Length Review
GET https://dev.villume.com/signup 200 OK text/html; charset=utf-8 112603 bytes HTTP shell loads successfully; signup flow is available after clicking Get started.

Code Review

File Review
src/app/layouts/header/header.component.html Desktop header contains the Get started button that opens the signup/login dialog flow.
src/app/layouts/layouts.component.html Shared layout includes <app-sign-up>, which renders the signup form inside the auth dialog.
src/app/layouts/sign-up/sign-up.component.html Required signup fields, consent copy, reCAPTCHA container, Create account button, password toggle, Google sign-up area, and Log in link are present.
src/app/layouts/sign-up/sign-up.component.ts Form validation exists, but password currently uses Validators.minLength(6). Issue #1600 requires minimum 8.
src/app/layouts/sign-up/sign-up.component.html Password placeholder says At least 6 characters and validation copy says Password must be at least 6 characters; both should match the issue requirement of minimum 8.

Bug Notes

  • Password minimum length does not match issue scope: implementation validates/copies 6 characters, while issue #1600 requires minimum 8.
  • reCAPTCHA markup/container is present, but the external widget was not visually rendered in the captured frame; Create account remains disabled until captcha verification.
  • Initial /signup screen shows the page shell/gallery content; the signup form appears after the expected Get started click.

Desktop screenshot - light mode:

Mobile screenshot - light mode:

issues.display_title: Issue #1636: [slicing] Add Help FAQ accordion and client-side search
issues.main_description_html:
Epic: Help

Add the FAQ accordion by reusing the existing app-faq component (inherits keyboard/aria behavior) with a Help-specific group, and wire the Help search box.

Decision (from brainstorming): search filters the on-page FAQ/topics only; the real searchable tutorial library is a separate content effort.

Acceptance: FAQ is keyboard-accessible; search filters FAQ/topics client-side; includes spec.

Depends on: Rebuild Help page on dashboard shell.

Part of the My Galleries authenticated-surface redesign.
Refs: docs/brainstorm/05-06-2026/my galleries/plan.md · brainstorming.md

QA Tester Summary - Issue #1636: Add Help FAQ accordion and client-side search

Suite: qa_testing/tests/e2e/help/issue_1636_help_faq_search.spec.ts (Playwright - Chromium) and qa_testing/scripts/record-issue-1636-dev-flow.cjs (Chrome headed/maximized - FFmpeg desktop recording)
Env: Development https://dev.villume.com
Flow: Open /help, click header FAQ link (href="/#faq"), then open each FAQ item one by one and verify component usage, search, light/dark, and responsive views.

Test Scenario & Result

# Box Scenario Given -> Expected Matrix Result
1 White help_route_response GET /help -> page shell responds successfully TP Pass
2 White faq_nav_entry click header FAQ link -> browser navigates to /#faq and FAQ section is visible TP Pass
3 White faq_component_reuse FAQ implementation should reuse existing <app-faq> with Help-specific group TP Fail
4 Gray faq_open_one_by_one open FAQ item 1 through 5 one by one -> selected item opens and previous item closes TP Pass
5 Gray faq_aria_markup FAQ buttons expose aria-expanded and aria-controls TP Pass
6 Gray help_search_filter Help search box filters FAQ/topics client-side TP Fail
7 Gray theme_support FAQ remains readable in light and dark mode Desktop light + desktop dark Pass
8 Gray responsive_mobile FAQ remains usable in mobile responsive viewport while Chrome stays maximized Mobile light + mobile dark Pass
9 Task Sprint #1636 acceptance FAQ is keyboard-accessible, search filters FAQ/topics client-side, and spec exists TP + FN Fail

Frontend Status & Review

Flow: https://dev.villume.com/help -> click header FAQ link -> https://dev.villume.com/#faq.

Area Expected Actual Review
FAQ entry link Header FAQ link opens FAQ section Verified: a.nav-link[href="/#faq"] navigates to /#faq Pass
FAQ render Help-specific FAQ available for task #1636 FAQ section renders on home route, not /help; /help itself shows tutorial video content Fail
Component reuse Reuse existing <app-faq> component Render state: hasAppFaq=false; home FAQ is inline markup in layouts/main Fail
FAQ aria Accordion buttons expose accessible relationships 5 visible FAQ buttons with aria-expanded and aria-controls Pass
One-by-one open FAQ items open sequentially Playwright spec and final video opened FAQ items 1 through 5 one by one Pass
Search Search box filters FAQ/topics on-page Render state: searchInputCount=0; no client-side FAQ/topic search found Fail
Visual states Light/dark and mobile states are captured Desktop/mobile screenshots and address-bar video captured Pass

Response Code & Review

Request Response Content-Type Review
GET https://dev.villume.com/help 200 OK text/html HTTP shell loads successfully, but #1636 acceptance content is incomplete.
GET https://dev.villume.com/#faq SPA route / hash navigation text/html FAQ section is reachable through header link, but it is not the Help-specific searchable FAQ requested by the issue.

Code Review

File Review
src/app/routes/static.routes.ts /help currently loads TutorialsComponent; it does not expose a Help FAQ/search page.
src/app/pages/tutorials/tutorials.component.html Help route content is a Creating an Exhibition video iframe; no <app-faq> and no search input.
src/app/layouts/header/header.component.html Header FAQ link is present as routerLink="/" fragment="faq", matching the user-provided button.
src/app/layouts/main/main.component.html FAQ section is implemented inline with .faq-list, not by reusing <app-faq>.
src/app/layouts/main/main.component.ts FAQ data is local faqs; accordion click/open behavior works one by one, but this is not wired to a Help-specific app-faq group or a search filter.
src/app/components/faq/faq.component.html Existing reusable component has accordion aria markup, but it is not used for the tested FAQ flow.

Bug Notes

  • Issue #1636 acceptance is not met: no Help-specific FAQ/search implementation was found.
  • Header FAQ button works and opens /#faq, but that FAQ is the home inline FAQ, not an <app-faq> reuse with Help-specific group.
  • FAQ open one by one works after clicking the header FAQ link, but the implementation still misses the issue's Help-specific app-faq reuse requirement.
  • No search input exists for filtering FAQ/topics client-side.

Mobile screenshot - light mode:

issues.display_title: Issue #1632: [slicing] Add My Galleries unit spec
issues.main_description_html:
Epic: My Galleries

Add main.component.spec.ts covering render + key interactions (search, sort, delete, bulk delete).

Acceptance: spec passes in CI; covers the new search/sort/bulk paths.

Depends on: the My Galleries implementation tasks.

Part of the My Galleries authenticated-surface redesign.
Refs: docs/brainstorm/05-06-2026/my galleries/plan.md · brainstorming.md

QA Tester Summary - Issue #1632: Add My Galleries unit spec

Suite: qa_testing/tests/e2e/my-galleries/issue_1632_unit_spec_coverage.spec.ts (Playwright source/coverage assertion) and qa_testing/scripts/record-issue-1632-dev-flow.cjs (Chrome headed/maximized - FFmpeg desktop recording)
Env: Development https://dev.villume.com/testergothru
Flow: Verify source coverage requirement for src/app/pages/profile/main/main.component.spec.ts, then capture My Galleries/Profile runtime evidence on dev.

Test Scenario & Result

# Box Scenario Given -> Expected Matrix Result
1 White unit_spec_exists repo contains src/app/pages/profile/main/main.component.spec.ts TP Fail
2 White render_spec_coverage unit spec covers component render/create behavior TP Fail
3 Gray search_spec_coverage unit spec covers My Galleries search path TP Fail
4 Gray sort_spec_coverage unit spec covers My Galleries sort path TP Fail
5 Gray delete_spec_coverage unit spec covers single gallery delete path TP Fail
6 Gray bulk_delete_spec_coverage unit spec covers selected/bulk delete path TP Fail
7 Gray runtime_controls_probe dev profile surface exposes search/sort/select/bulk delete controls Desktop + mobile Fail
8 Gray theme_responsive_evidence capture light/dark and mobile/desktop evidence while Chrome stays maximized Desktop light/dark + mobile light/dark Pass
9 Task Sprint #1632 acceptance spec passes in CI and covers new search/sort/bulk paths TP + FN Fail

Frontend Status & Review

Flow: https://dev.villume.com/testergothru.

Area Expected Actual Review
Unit spec file src/app/pages/profile/main/main.component.spec.ts exists File not found Fail
Render coverage Spec covers render/create behavior Not possible because spec file is absent Fail
Search coverage Spec covers search path Not found in spec; implementation probe also found searchInputCount=0 Fail
Sort coverage Spec covers sort path Not found in spec; implementation probe also found sortControlCount=0 Fail
Delete coverage Spec covers delete path Component has single confirmDelete/deleteExhibition, but no unit spec exists Fail
Bulk delete coverage Spec covers selected/bulk delete path Selection state exists in TS, but template only exposes Clear/Share; no bulk delete button/path found Fail
Runtime evidence My Galleries/Profile surface captured Runtime loaded as public Galleries because recorder login attempt did not open login form; source-level spec failure remains conclusive Partial
Evidence Video records visible Chrome window including address bar Verified: address bar shows dev.villume.com/testergothru Pass

Response Code & Review

Request Response Content-Type Body Length Review
GET https://dev.villume.com/testergothru 200 OK text/html; charset=utf-8 112603 bytes HTTP shell loads successfully; unit-spec acceptance is still missing in source.

Code Review

File Review
src/app/pages/profile/main/main.component.spec.ts Missing. This directly fails the issue acceptance.
src/app/pages/profile/main/main.component.ts Contains render/data/delete-related logic and selectedIds, but no search/sort methods were found.
src/app/pages/profile/main/main.component.html Renders gallery cards and single delete actions; no visible search input, sort control, gallery checkbox, or bulk delete action was found.
src/app/pages/profile/main/main.component.scss Contains .gcheck and .dash-selbar styles, but the current template does not expose a checkbox control or bulk delete action.
qa_testing/tests/e2e/my-galleries/issue_1632_unit_spec_coverage.spec.ts Added QA guard that fails until the required unit spec and coverage paths exist.

Bug Notes

  • Issue #1632 acceptance is not met because main.component.spec.ts is absent.
  • Search, sort, and bulk delete paths requested by the issue were not found in the current My Galleries component/template.
  • Runtime recorder could not authenticate as owner during this pass, so the screenshot/video show public Galleries; the source/unit-spec failure is independent of login state.

Type: Slicing (frontend build) · Epic: Settings

Rebuild account-setting.component on the new design system.

Scope: private email; Change password disclosure (current/new/confirm with show/hide toggles); birth date; time zone; Gender as a proper radio group; Save; danger zone → delete account (keep confirm flow).

Defect fix: gender currently uses (click) on <label> instead of real radio binding — bind to a reactive radio group.

Decision: keep the existing 6-character password minimum (no policy change here).

Acceptance: fields render + save; gender bound correctly; change-password disclosure works; delete-account confirm intact.

Depends on: _Add Settings Styles To dashboard.scss.

Part of the Settings & Password-Recovery redesign (Frontend).
Refs: docs/brainstorm/05-06-2026/ui-setting-email-password/plan.md · brainstorming.md

Demo by Developer

Account: testergothru@gmail.com

4 Gray password_minimum_copy password form -> 6-character minimum remains visible/enforced TP Pass
5 Gray delete_confirm_flow click Delete account -> confirm dialog appears and cancel keeps account intact TP Pass
6 Gray responsive_theme Account Settings remains usable in desktop/mobile light/dark views Desktop + mobile Pass

Area Expected Actual Review
Account settings fields Private email, birth date, time zone, gender, save button, and danger zone render Verified on desktop and mobile screenshots Pass
Change password Disclosure opens with password fields and show/hide controls Verified in final video and mobile dark screenshot Pass

Delete account confirm Confirm flow remains intact Verified: modal copy Delete this account? with Delete account and Cancel actions appears Pass
Gender binding Reactive radio group Current Account Settings uses [(ngModel)]="selectedGender" on p-radioButton Fail
Responsive views Chrome maximized, web viewport emulates mobile Verified with desktop/mobile light/dark screenshots and address-bar video Pass

GET https://dev.villume.com/settings/account 200 OK text/html; charset=utf-8 Route shell loads successfully with authenticated UI after login.

Epic B — Gallery wizard · Step 1 CreateGallery

Slice Step 1 (choose a space) — also builds the shared wizard shell (logged-in nav, sticky action bar, step progress "Step 1 of 3").

Scope

  • Space grid as radio cards (badge Modifiable/Fixed + selected check); filter chips (All/Modifiable/Fixed) + result count.
  • Sticky action bar: hint, Skip, Continue (disabled until selection).
  • Light + dark; responsive. Markup only.

Acceptance criteria

  • Matches CreateGallery.html in both themes, all breakpoints.

Testing scenario (QA)

  1. Compare to CreateGallery.html on desktop & mobile.
  2. Select a card → selected state shows; Continue enables.
  3. Dark mode + responsive grid reflow correct.

QA Tester Summary - Issue #1604: CreateGallery Step 1 (/create-exhibition)

Suite: qa_testing/tests/e2e/create-gallery/issue_1604_create_gallery_step1.spec.ts (Playwright - Chromium) and qa_testing/scripts/record-issue-1604-dev-flow.cjs (Chrome headed/maximized - FFmpeg desktop recording)

issues.display_title: Issue #1599: [Integration] Login (/login) — real auth, Google, redirect
issues.main_description_html:
Epic A — Auth · Login (/login)

Wire Login to the real backend.

Scope

  • Real auth service (replace the prototype's simulated submit).
  • Real Google OAuth; "Forgot password?" → /forgot-password; "Sign up" → /signup.
  • Validation + error states; honor existing post-login redirect.

Acceptance criteria

  • Valid creds log in and redirect correctly; invalid creds show errors; Google login works.

Testing scenario (QA)

  1. Log in with valid credentials → authenticated + redirected as expected.
  2. Wrong password → inline error; no navigation.
  3. Empty fields → validation blocks submit.
  4. "Log in with Google" → real OAuth completes.
  5. "Forgot password?" → /forgot-password.

Demo by Developer

2026-06-09.17-11-39.mp4

QA Tester Summary - Issue #1599: Login (/login) real auth, Google, redirect

Suite: qa_testing/tests/e2e/auth/issue_1599_login_integration.spec.ts (Playwright - Chromium) and qa_testing/scripts/record-issue-1599-dev-flow.cjs (Chrome headed/maximized - FFmpeg desktop recording)
Env: Development https://dev.villume.com
Account: testergothru@gmail.com

Test Scenario & Result

# Box Scenario Given -> Expected Matrix Result
1 White login_route /login -> Login page/form renders TP Fail
2 White valid_credentials valid credentials -> authenticated and redirected TP Pass
3 Gray invalid_password wrong password -> error and no authenticated navigation TP Pass
4 Gray empty_fields empty submit -> validation blocks submit and shows error states TP Partial
5 Gray forgot_password_link Forgot password -> /forgot-password TP Fail
6 Gray signup_link Sign up -> /signup TP Fail
7 Gray google_login_entry Google login is real OAuth entry TP Partial
8 Gray responsive_theme Login/sign-up modal works in desktop/mobile light/dark Desktop + mobile Pass
9 Task Sprint #1599 acceptance /login real auth, Google OAuth, redirect, validation, forgot/signup routes TP + FN Fail

Frontend Status & Review

Area Expected Actual Review
/login route Dedicated login page/form /login responds 200 OK but renders a Galleries shell, not #login-email / #login-pw Fail
Valid login Real auth service logs in and redirects Valid tester credentials log in and redirect to /testergothru Pass
Invalid login Inline/error state and no navigation Wrong password keeps login modal open and displays login failure messaging Pass
Empty fields Validation blocks submit and shows required field errors Continue is disabled, so submit is blocked; required error messages are not surfaced by clicking disabled submit Partial
Forgot password Link navigates to /forgot-password Link opens forgot-password modal on /, not route navigation Fail
Sign up Link navigates to /signup Link switches modal to sign-up form on /; /signup route does not render sign-up form Fail
Google OAuth Real OAuth completes Google Identity button/widget renders; full OAuth completion was not executed because it requires an interactive Google account session Partial

Response Code & Review

Request Response Content-Type Review
GET https://dev.villume.com/login 200 OK text/html; charset=utf-8 Route shell responds, but does not contain the login form expected by issue scope.
GET https://dev.villume.com/forgot-password 200 OK text/html; charset=utf-8 Forgot-password route exists and renders forgot content, but login modal link does not navigate there.
GET https://dev.villume.com/signup 200 OK text/html; charset=utf-8 Route shell responds, but no sign-up form/page content was detected.
POST auth via UI Success with valid tester credentials Authenticated UI Valid login flow works and redirects to /testergothru.

Code Review

File Review
src/app/routes/auth.routes.ts:5 Only forgot-password, reset-password, and resettoken auth routes are defined; no /login or /signup route is present.
src/app/layouts/login/login.component.html:6 Login modal includes #login-email.
src/app/layouts/login/login.component.html:33 Login modal includes #login-pw.
src/app/layouts/login/login.component.html:27 Forgot password link calls component action, not route navigation.
src/app/layouts/login/login.component.ts:58 Login uses mainService.auth(this.form.value), so real backend auth is wired for credential login.
src/app/layouts/login/login.component.ts:112 Verified login redirects to /<username> when no special login source is active.
src/app/layouts/login/login.component.ts:163 forgotPassword() opens the modal forgot-password flow by setting displayForgotPasswordDialog, rather than routing to /forgot-password.
src/app/layouts/layouts.component.html:70 Login UI is embedded inside a dialog (Welcome back), not a /login page.
src/app/layouts/layouts.component.html:100 Sign up link switches modal form type, not route navigation to /signup.
src/app/layouts/layouts.component.ts:379 Google Identity renderButton is used, confirming real Google widget rendering.

Bug Notes

  • Issue #1599 acceptance is not fully met because /login and /signup do not render dedicated auth pages.
  • Forgot password link from login does not navigate to /forgot-password; it opens a modal on /.
  • Empty fields are blocked by disabled Continue, but required error text does not appear from an empty submit attempt.
  • Credential login itself is functional: valid credentials authenticate and invalid password shows an error.
  • Google widget renders, but full OAuth completion was not tested without an interactive Google test account.
  • Because explicit scope items fail, add labels Bug, Sanity Testing, and assign ekosy9.
